data.content:
### File System Watching API

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.5.0/sandbox_sync/page.mdx

API for watching directory events.

```APIDOC
## POST /e2b-dev/e2b/watch_dir

### Description
Watch directory for filesystem events.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/e2b-dev/e2b/watch_dir

### Parameters
#### Query Parameters
- **path** (str) - Required - Path to a directory to watch
- **user** (Username) - Optional - Run the operation as this user (default: "user")
- **request_timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the request in seconds
- **recursive** (bool) - Optional - Watch directory recursively (default: false)

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **WatchHandle** (object) - Object for stopping watching directory
```

--------------------------------

### Directory Watching

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.8.0/sandbox_async/page.mdx

API for watching directories for filesystem events.

```APIDOC
## POST /fs/watch_dir

### Description
Watch directory for filesystem events.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/fs/watch_dir

### Parameters
#### Request Body
- **path** (string) - Required - Path to a directory to watch
- **on_event** (function) - Required - Callback to call on each event in the directory
- **on_exit** (function) - Optional - Callback to call when the watching ends
- **user** (string) - Optional - Run the operation as this user (default: "user")
- **request_timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the request in seconds
- **timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the watch operation in seconds. Using `0` will not limit the watch time (default: 60)
- **recursive** (boolean) - Optional - Watch directory recursively (default: false)

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **watch_handle** (object) - An object to stop watching the directory
  - **stop** (function) - Function to stop watching the directory

### Request Example
```json
{
  "path": "/watched/directory",
  "recursive": true
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Filesystem Watcher API

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.11.0/sandbox_sync/page.mdx

API endpoints for watching directory changes and retrieving filesystem events.

```APIDOC
## POST /files/watch_dir

### Description
Watch directory for filesystem events.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
`/files/watch_dir`

### Parameters
#### Query Parameters
- **path** (str) - Required - Path to a directory to watch
- **user** (Username) - Optional - Run the operation as this user (default: "user")
- **request_timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the request in seconds
- **recursive** (bool) - Optional - Watch directory recursively (default: false)

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **WatchHandle** (object) - Object for stopping watching directory.

## DELETE /files/watch_dir/stop

### Description
Stop watching the directory.

### Method
DELETE

### Endpoint
`/files/watch_dir/stop`

### Parameters
#### Query Parameters
- **watch_id** (str) - Required - The ID of the watch handle to stop.

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **null** - Indicates the watcher has been stopped.

## GET /files/watch_dir/events

### Description
Get the latest events that have occurred in the watched directory since the last call, or from the beginning of the watching, up until now.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
`/files/watch_dir/events`

### Parameters
#### Query Parameters
- **watch_id** (str) - Required - The ID of the watch handle to get events from.

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **List[FilesystemEvent]** (array) - List of filesystem events.

#### Response Example
```json
[
  {
    "type": "create",
    "path": "/new_file.txt"
  },
  {
    "type": "rename",
    "oldPath": "/old_name.txt",
    "newPath": "/new_name.txt"
  }
]
```
```

--------------------------------

### Sandbox Management API

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.8.0/sandbox_async/page.mdx

APIs for managing sandbox settings and retrieving sandbox information.

```APIDOC
## POST /sandbox/set_timeout

### Description
Set the timeout of the specified sandbox. After the timeout expires the sandbox will be automatically killed. This method can extend or reduce the sandbox timeout set when creating the sandbox or from the last call to `.set_timeout`.

Maximum time a sandbox can be kept alive is 24 hours (86_400 seconds) for Pro users and 1 hour (3_600 seconds) for Hobby users.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/sandbox/set_timeout

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
None

#### Query Parameters
- **sandbox_id** (str) - Required - Sandbox ID
- **timeout** (int) - Required - Timeout for the sandbox in seconds
- **api_key** (str) - Optional - E2B API Key to use for authentication
- **domain** (str) - Optional - E2B domain to use for authentication
- **debug** (bool) - Optional - Whether to use debug mode
- **request_timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the request in seconds
- **proxy** (ProxyTypes) - Optional - Proxy to use for the request

#### Request Body
None

### Request Example
```json
{
  "sandbox_id": "your_sandbox_id",
  "timeout": 3600
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **None** - Indicates the timeout was successfully set.

#### Response Example
(No response body on success)
```

```APIDOC
## GET /sandbox/get_info

### Description
Get sandbox information like sandbox ID, template, metadata, started at/end at date.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
/sandbox/get_info

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
None

#### Query Parameters
- **request_timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the request in seconds
- **sandbox_id** (str) - Optional - Sandbox ID (if not provided, uses the current sandbox context)
- **api_key** (str) - Optional - E2B API Key to use for authentication
- **domain** (str) - Optional - E2B domain to use for authentication
- **debug** (bool) - Optional - Whether to use debug mode
- **headers** (Dict[str, str]) - Optional - Custom headers to use for the request
- **proxy** (ProxyTypes) - Optional - Proxy to use for the request

#### Request Body
None

### Request Example
```json
{
  "request_timeout": 10.0
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **SandboxInfo** (object) - Information about the sandbox.
  - `sandbox_id` (str) - The ID of the sandbox.
  - `template` (str) - The template used for the sandbox.
  - `metadata` (object) - Additional metadata for the sandbox.
  - `started_at` (str) - The timestamp when the sandbox was started (ISO 8601 format).
  - `ended_at` (str) - The timestamp when the sandbox was ended (ISO 8601 format), null if still running.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "sandbox_id": "sandbox_123abc",
  "template": "ubuntu-22.04",
  "metadata": {},
  "started_at": "2023-10-27T10:00:00Z",
  "ended_at": null
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Sandbox Management API

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.7.1/sandbox_async/page.mdx

API endpoints for managing the sandbox environment, including checking its status.

```APIDOC
## GET /sandbox/is_running

### Description
Check if the sandbox is running.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
/sandbox/is_running

### Parameters
#### Query Parameters
- **request_timeout** (Optional[float]) - Optional - Timeout for the request in seconds

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **running** (bool) - True if the sandbox is running, False otherwise

### Response Example
```json
{
  "running": true
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Directory Watching

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.11.0/sandbox_async/page.mdx

API for watching directories for filesystem events and managing the watch process.

```APIDOC
## POST /fs/watch_dir

### Description
Watch directory for filesystem events.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/fs/watch_dir

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
-
#### Query Parameters
- **path** (str) - Required - Path to a directory to watch
- **user** (Username) - Optional - Run the operation as this user (default: "user")
- **request_timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the request in seconds
- **timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the watch operation in seconds. Using `0` will not limit the watch time (default: 60)
- **recursive** (bool) - Optional - Watch directory recursively (default: false)

### Request Example
```json
{
  "path": "/watched/directory",
  "user": "user",
  "request_timeout": 30.0,
  "timeout": 0,
  "recursive": true
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **result** (AsyncWatchHandle) - Object for stopping watching directory

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "result": {
    "watch_id": "some-watch-id"
  }
}
```
```

```APIDOC
## POST /fs/watch_dir/stop

### Description
Stop watching a directory.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/fs/watch_dir/stop

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
-
#### Query Parameters
- **watch_id** (str) - Required - The ID of the watch to stop

### Request Example
```json
{
  "watch_id": "some-watch-id"
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **result** (None) - Operation successful

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "result": null
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Command Execution API

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.11.0/sandbox_async/page.mdx

APIs for listing, killing, sending stdin, and running commands within the sandbox.

```APIDOC
## GET /commands/list

### Description
Lists all running commands and PTY sessions.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
/commands/list

### Parameters
#### Query Parameters
- **request_timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the request in seconds

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **List[ProcessInfo]** - List of running commands and PTY sessions
```

```APIDOC
## DELETE /commands/{pid}

### Description
Kills a running command specified by its process ID using the `SIGKILL` signal.

### Method
DELETE

### Endpoint
/commands/{pid}

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **pid** (int) - Required - Process ID of the command to kill. Use `sandbox.commands.list()` to get the list of processes.

#### Query Parameters
- **request_timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the request in seconds

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **bool** - `True` if the command was killed, `False` if the command was not found
```

```APIDOC
## POST /commands/{pid}/send_stdin

### Description
Sends data to the standard input (stdin) of a running command.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/commands/{pid}/send_stdin

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **pid** (int) - Required - Process ID of the command. Use `sandbox.commands.list()` to get the list of processes.

#### Query Parameters
- **data** (string) - Required - Data to send to the command's stdin
- **request_timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the request in seconds

### Request Example
```json
{
  "data": "input data",
  "request_timeout": 5.0
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- None
```

```APIDOC
## POST /commands/run

### Description
Starts a new command and waits until it finishes executing.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/commands/run

### Parameters
#### Query Parameters
- **cmd** (string) - Required - Command to execute
- **background** (boolean) - Optional - `False` if the command should be executed in the foreground (default), `True` if in the background.
- **envs** (Dict[str, str]) - Optional - Environment variables to use for the command
- **user** (Username) - Optional - User to run the command as (defaults to "user")
- **cwd** (string) - Optional - Working directory to run the command
- **on_stdout** (OutputHandler[Stdout]) - Optional - Callback for command stdout output
- **on_stderr** (OutputHandler[Stderr]) - Optional - Callback for command stderr output
- **timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the command connection in seconds. Using `0` will not limit the command connection time (defaults to 60).
- **request_timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the request in seconds

### Request Example
```json
{
  "cmd": "ls -l",
  "background": false,
  "user": "testuser",
  "cwd": "/app"
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **CommandResult** (object) - Result of the command execution
```

--------------------------------

### Sandbox Management API

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.3.5/sandbox_async/page.mdx

API endpoints for creating, managing, and checking the status of E2B sandboxes.

```APIDOC
## POST /v1/sandbox/create

### Description
Create a new E2B cloud sandbox environment.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/v1/sandbox/create

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
None

#### Query Parameters
None

#### Request Body
- **template** (string) - Optional - The name or ID of the sandbox template to use (defaults to 'base').
- **timeout** (integer) - Optional - The maximum duration the sandbox will remain active in seconds. Default is 300 seconds. Maximum limits apply based on user subscription.
- **metadata** (object) - Optional - A key-value map for custom metadata associated with the sandbox.
- **envs** (object) - Optional - A key-value map for custom environment variables to set within the sandbox.
- **api_key** (string) - Optional - Your E2B API key. Defaults to the `E2B_API_KEY` environment variable.
- **domain** (string) - Optional - The domain to use for the sandbox.
- **debug** (boolean) - Optional - Enable or disable debug mode for the sandbox.
- **request_timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the sandbox creation request in seconds.
- **proxy** (object) - Optional - Proxy configuration to use for the request and subsequent sandbox network traffic.

### Request Example
```json
{
  "template": "python-3.10",
  "timeout": 1800,
  "metadata": {
    "project": "my-app"
  },
  "envs": {
    "NODE_ENV": "production"
  }
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **sandboxId** (string) - The unique identifier of the newly created sandbox.
- **sandboxUrl** (string) - The URL to access the sandbox.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "sandboxId": "sb-abcdef1234567890",
  "sandboxUrl": "https://sandbox.e2b.dev/sb-abcdef1234567890"
}
```

## GET /v1/sandbox/{sandboxId}/is_running

### Description
Check if a specific sandbox is currently running.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
/v1/sandbox/{sandboxId}/is_running

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **sandboxId** (string) - Required - The unique identifier of the sandbox to check.

#### Query Parameters
- **request_timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the request in seconds.

### Request Example
```json
{
  "sandboxId": "sb-abcdef1234567890"
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **running** (boolean) - `True` if the sandbox is running, `False` otherwise.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "running": true
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Directory Watching API

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.2.1/sandbox_async/page.mdx

API endpoint for watching a directory for filesystem events.

```APIDOC
## POST /fs/watch_dir

### Description
Watch a specified directory for filesystem events and receive event notifications via a callback.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/fs/watch_dir

### Parameters
#### Query Parameters
- **path** (string) - Required - Path to the directory to watch.
- **user** (string) - Optional - Run the operation as this user (default: "user").
- **request_timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the request in seconds.
- **timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the watch operation in seconds. Use `0` for no time limit (default: 60).
- **recursive** (boolean) - Optional - Watch the directory recursively (default: false).

### Request Body
*Note: The `on_event` and `on_exit` parameters are typically handled via callbacks or webhooks in a real API scenario. For this documentation, we represent them conceptually.* 

#### Request Body Fields
- **on_event** (callback) - Required - Callback function to be executed when a filesystem event occurs. It receives a `FilesystemEvent` object.
- **on_exit** (callback) - Optional - Callback function to be executed when the watching ends, receiving an `Exception` object if an error occurred.

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **watch_handle** (object) - An object representing the active watch session, which can be used to stop watching.
  - **id** (string) - Unique identifier for the watch handle.

### Response Example
```json
{
  "watch_handle": {
    "id": "watch-12345"
  }
}
```
```
